commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: 1 Esdras Wisdom of Solomon Wisdom of Sirach Epistle of Jeremiah Prayer of Manasseh O Israel, return unto the LORD your God; for you have fallen because of your iniquity. Perish, because she hath stirred up her God to bitterness. It is not a curse or imprecation, but a prophecy of what should come to pass (Challoner) to Israel, in Assyria. Many such expressions occur, Psalm lxviii. Jerome) Sometimes they are the effects of zeal, conformable to divine justice, Psalm cxl. (Worthington) Hebrew, "Samaria has sinned, or shall perish. "(Calmet) Bitterness. Septuagint, "she hath resisted her God.

commentaries.author: Richard Challoner
commentaries.content: Perish, because she hath stirred up her God to bitterness: It is not a curse or imprecation, but a prophecy of what should come to pass.
